symptoms: engine won't crank just clicks, clicking noise, no power at all dashboard is dark, dead battery
diagnostic_procedures: Test battery voltage (should be 12.6V resting). Perform load test. Check battery terminals for corrosion. Inspect cables for breaks.
repair_outcomes: Replaced battery successfully. Cleaned terminals. Vehicle starts normally.
maintenance_knowledge: Replace battery every 3-5 years. Clean terminals annually. Avoid short trips that prevent full charging.

symptoms: car dies while driving, dim lights, battery light is on dashboard, whining noise from engine bay low voltage
diagnostic_procedures: Test alternator output (should be 13.5V-14.5V running). Inspect drive belt tension and condition. Check for loose connections at alternator.
repair_outcomes: Replaced alternator and drive belt. Charging system restored.
maintenance_knowledge: Inspect drive belt every 30,000 miles. Don't ignore battery warning lights.

symptoms: engine shaking loss of power, rough idle check engine light flashing, stuttering on highway hesitating, jerking acceleration bad vibration, weird engine noise knocking sound, engine noise loud rumbling
diagnostic_procedures: Pull DTC codes. Inspect spark plugs for fouling or wear. Check ignition coil resistance. Test fuel injector pulse. Perform compression test if needed.
repair_outcomes: Replaced spark plugs and one faulty ignition coil. Engine runs smoothly.
maintenance_knowledge: Replace spark plugs according to owner's manual (usually every 30,000-100,000 miles). Use high-quality fuel.

symptoms: bad smell poor fuel economy rotten egg, lack of power exhaust smell sulfur, check engine light bad mileage sluggish
diagnostic_procedures: Check for P0420/P0430 codes. Measure exhaust backpressure. Use an infrared thermometer to check inlet vs outlet temps.
maintenance_knowledge: Fix engine misfires immediately to prevent unburned fuel from destroying the catalytic converter.

symptoms: hesitation upon acceleration rough idle, poor gas mileage running lean, slow response from engine poor fuel efficiency
diagnostic_procedures: Monitor O2 sensor voltage via OBD-II live data. Should oscillate between 0.1V and 0.9V rapidly. Check for vacuum leaks.
repair_outcomes: Replaced upstream O2 sensor. Fuel trims returned to normal.
maintenance_knowledge: O2 sensors degrade over time. Replacement around 100,000 miles often improves fuel economy.

symptoms: squealing noise when braking high pitch, grinding noise from wheels when stopping, brake pedal feels soft takes long to stop, screeching sound when I press brakes
diagnostic_procedures: Remove wheels and inspect brake pad thickness. Check rotors for scoring or warping. Inspect calipers for leaks.
repair_outcomes: Installed new brake pads and resurfaced rotors. Braking power restored, no noise.
maintenance_knowledge: Have brakes inspected every 10,000 miles or during tire rotations. Flush brake fluid every 2 years.

symptoms: engine overheats quickly sweet smell, temperature gauge high steam from hood, puddle of green yellow liquid under car
diagnostic_procedures: Perform cooling system pressure test. Inspect radiator, hoses, and water pump for visible leaks. Check oil for milky appearance (head gasket check).
repair_outcomes: Replaced leaking upper radiator hose and topped off coolant. Temperature stable.
maintenance_knowledge: Flush and replace engine coolant according to schedule (usually every 50,000 miles). Check hoses for cracking.

symptoms: vibration when driving high speed steering shakes, pulling to one side drifts left or right, steering wheel is crooked uneven tire wear
diagnostic_procedures: Check tire pressure. Inspect suspension components for wear (tie rods, ball joints). Place vehicle on alignment rack and measure camber, caster, and toe.
repair_outcomes: Performed 4-wheel alignment. Vehicle tracks straight, steering wheel centered.
maintenance_knowledge: Get an alignment when buying new tires, or if you hit a severe pothole/curb.

symptoms: AC not blowing cold air at all, warm air from AC vents hissing sound, air conditioner stopped working suddenly
diagnostic_procedures: Connect AC manifold gauges to check high/low pressures. Use UV light to find dye leaks, or an electronic sniffer detector.
repair_outcomes: Found leak at condenser. Replaced condenser, vacuumed system, and recharged with R134a.
maintenance_knowledge: Run the AC periodically even in winter to keep compressor seals lubricated.

symptoms: gears slipping won't shift up, delayed shifting hard shift clunking, car revs high but moves slowly, transmission fluid leak smells burnt
diagnostic_procedures: Check transmission fluid level and condition. Scan TCM for fault codes. Perform stall test and road test.
repair_outcomes: Replaced shift solenoid and performed fluid flush. Shifting returned to normal.
maintenance_knowledge: Change transmission fluid every 30,000-60,000 miles. Don't ignore hard shifting or fluid leaks.
